nodes.name: Error Trigger
nodes.notes:
1. The catch-net.

This node fires whenever ANY other workflow in this project hits an
unrecoverable error. WF1 and WF2 both declare this workflow as their
Error Workflow in Settings, so their failures funnel here.
Input = an object with { workflow, execution, error } describing what blew up.

nodes.name: Format Error Message
nodes.notes:
2. Turn the raw error into a reviewer-readable note.

Trims the stack trace, pulls the failing node name, and builds a short
DM body. The 'Logs:' link points at localhost (owner only) because the
cloudflared tunnel is scoped to /webhook/*, not the UI.
If we ever swap chat_id to a public user, strip this line.

nodes.name: Send Error to Telegram
nodes.notes:
3. Deliver to the owner.

chat_id is hardcoded to 6272541552 (bot owner). Unauthorized users
who trip WF1/WF2's allowlist get a silent experience, only the owner
sees 'someone tried X'. No info leak to the attacker.
If the bot is ever opened up, pipe chat_id from the errored execution.
